target.name: Shield AI

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $12.7B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $3.0B - $61.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Flock Safety
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series G), post-money $7.5B

data.narrative:
Shield AI, a Series G defense and security platform founded by Brandon Tseng, Ryan Tseng, and Andrew Reiter, carries a post-money valuation of $12.7B following $1.5B+ in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 8 private comparables in the Defense & Security sector alongside 2 public reference points, with notable private peers including Anduril Industries ($61.0B, Series H), Helsing ($18.0B, Series D), and Flock Safety ($7.5B, Series G).

Two valuation methods were triangulated. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.25x multiplier typical of Series G advancement, yielding $15.9B. Peer-median methodology across 8 same-sector comparables generates $7.8B, reflecting a peer range of $3.0B to $61.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in funding stage and technology maturity within the Defense & Security landscape.

Synthesizing both approaches produces a valuation range of $6.6B to $18.3B, with a median of $11.8B. The current $12.7B valuation sits comfortably within this band and modestly above the peer median, consistent with Shield AI's later-stage funding and capital intensity relative to the broader cohort. Key uncertainty stems from the substantial valuation spread among comparable firms, which suggests stage and technology differentiation are material drivers. Additional resolution would benefit from revenue multiples or growth rate normalization across the peer set.
